Details: Removal of Cryptosporidium in water by nanofiltration with a polyamide composite thin film (CTF) was found to be very effective for one study (>6-log removal or about three orders of magnitude greater removal than achieved with conventional treatment). In the same study, nanofiltration with a membrane that had a cellulose acetate (CA) thin film was significantly less effective in removing Cryptosporidium (2 to 3-log removal).
